'''Sam Stone''' (born 4 August 1997) is a [[Malta national rugby league team|Malta]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] and [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Stone was born on the [[Gold Coast, Queensland]], [[Australia]] and is of Maltese descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.totalrl.com/sammut-malta-mix-autumn-internationals/ |title=Sammut in Malta mix for Autumn internationals |publisher=totalrl.com |date=7 September 2016 |accessdate=7 September 2016}}</ref> He moved to [[Newcastle, New South Wales]] at a young age.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Valentine-Eleebana Red Devils]] and [[Lakes United]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].

Stone is the son of former [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] coach [[Rick Stone]].<ref>{{cite web|author=ROBERT DILLON1 Feb 2017, 6 p.m.|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/4441483/knights-rookie-a-chip-off-old-stone-block/|title=Knights rookie a chip off old Stone block | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2017-02-01|access-date=2017-02-28}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2015 and 2016, Stone played for the Newcastle Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131072555/http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=31 January 2016|title=S|publisher=Nyc Database|date=27 December 2012|accessdate=2017-02-28}}</ref>

===2017===
In round 1 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Stone made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Knights against the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-warriors-v-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/104285/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Warriors v Knights |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |accessdate=2017-03-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2017/03/05/late_mail_warriors_v.af_news.html |title=LATE MAIL: Warriors v Knights - Knights |publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au |date= |accessdate=2017-03-05}}</ref> In May, he played for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-team-named/tabid/10871/newsid/106280/default.aspx |title=Junior Kangaroos team named |publisher=NRL.com |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-02 |archive-date=19 August 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170819144738/https://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-team-named/tabid/10871/newsid/106280/default.aspx |url-status=dead }}</ref> He finished his debut season with 16 NRL matches and two tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMjM0MDUtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=26 June 2019}}</ref> In September, he had his Knights contract extended until the end of 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/luke-yates-re-signs-knights-29414/|title=Luke Yates re-signs with Knights - Zero Tackle|date=27 September 2017|publisher=|accessdate=27 September 2017}}</ref>

===2018===
Stone only appeared in two NRL matches for the Knights in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTcxNy00LS0tLS0tMjM0MDUtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=26 June 2019}}</ref> Late in the year, he played for [[Malta national rugby league team|Malta]] in the [[2018 Emerging Nations World Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rlenwc.com/2018/09/22/malta-name-squad-for-emerging-nations-world-championship-opener/|title=Malta name squad for Emerging Nations World Championship opener - Rugby League Emerging Nations World Championship|date=22 September 2018|publisher=|accessdate=5 October 2018|archive-date=24 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180924145538/https://www.rlenwc.com/2018/09/22/malta-name-squad-for-emerging-nations-world-championship-opener/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2019===
After round 14, Stone moved to the [[Gold Coast Titans]] mid-season on a contract until the end of 2020, after failing to make an NRL appearance for the Knights so far that season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2019/06/25/titans-sign-knights-forward/|title=Titans sign Knights forward|date=25 June 2019|website=Gold Coast Titans|accessdate=26 June 2019}}</ref>
Stone made eight appearances for the Gold Coast in 2019 as the club finished last on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/06/humiliated-and-appalled-proctors-apology-to-titans-fans/|title=Proctor's apology to Titans fans|website=NRL|date=6 August 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-melbourne-storm-defeat-gold-coast-titans-after-scare/news-story/8b111ca8f928e7f74c956e68be0394ed/|title=Melbourne Storm defeat Gold Coast Titans after scare|newspaper=News.com.au|date=25 August 2019|last1=McMurtry|first1=Andrew}}</ref>

===2020===
Stone played nine games for the Gold Coast in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/nrl-2020-nrl-contract-news-signings-transfers-tyrone-roberts-leaves-gold-coast-titans/news-story/fc243b6b9be9fa4e9cf98cd4c2e8d616/|title= Former captain Tyrone Roberts shown the door by Gold Coast Titans|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date= 12 October 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
On 2 August 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=2 Aug 2021|accessdate=3 Aug 2021|title=Sam Stone & Jai Whitbread: Leigh Centurions sign Gold Coast Titans pair|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58063318}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/news-2021-gold-coast-titans-ash-taylor-tyrone-peachey-contracts/b6fc2716-8e77-4cff-9a2a-20f8b6bcb13c/|title=Big-name Gold Coast playmakers Ash Taylor, Tyrone Peachey among off-season Titans discards|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===2022===
On 3 October, Stone played for Leigh in their [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] which saw the club promoted back to the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12710793/leigh-44-12-batley-centurions-seal-super-league-return-with-deserved-win-in-championship-grand-final/|title=Leigh 44-12 Batley: Centurions seal Super League return with deserved win in Championship Grand Final|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
On 7 November, Stone signed a contract to join [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] ahead of the 2023 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-stone-salford-sign-leigh-forward/|title=Salford confirm signing of Leigh forward Sam Stone|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===2023===
In round 25 of the 2023 Super League season, Stone scored a try in golden point extra-time to win the game for Salford 24-20 against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/sep/10/salford-red-devils-warrington-wolves-super-league-match-report/|title=Salford get playoff boost as Sam Stone's sudden-death try sinks Warrington|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
In the 2023 Super League season, Stone played a total of 23 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk}}</ref>

===2025===
During the period of financial turmoil at [[Salford Red Devils]], on 15 June 2025 it was reported that he had refused to play in the match against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and insisted that he will neither train nor play for the club ever again; for which he was roundly criticised by the head-coach [[Paul Rowley]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Jun 2025|accessdate=16 Jun 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Paul Rowley slams Sam Stone’s refusal to play in damning assessment of star|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/paul-rowley-slams-sam-stones-refusal-to-play-in-damning-assessment-of-star}}</ref>

On 17 June, it was reported that he had signed for Warrington in the Super League on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Jun 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Warrington duo join Salford Red Devils as Sam Stone swap complete|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-duo-join-salford-red-devils-as-sam-stone-swap-complete}}</ref>
He made his debut for Warrington in a 16-24 defeat against Huddersfield the following week.<ref> https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4678582 </ref>
